macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed Maintains Interest Rates Amid Middle East Uncertainty
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell announced the committee decided to maintain the target range for the federal funds rate at 3.5% to 3.75%, citing economic uncertainty from the Middle East conflict and higher energy prices pushing up inflation.

items.title: Gas Prices Surge Over 9 Cents in Single Day
items.summary: The AAA national average price for regular gasoline jumped more than nine cents, rising from $4.30 to $4.392, reflecting broader energy market pressures.

items.title: Mortgage Rates Rise to 6.3%
items.summary: The average rate on a 30-year fixed mortgage increased to 6.3% this week, up from 6.23% the previous week, according to Freddie Mac data.

items.title: U.S. National Debt Surpasses 100% of GDP
items.summary: The U.S. national debt held by the public has exceeded the size of the economy for the first time since World War II, reaching over 100% of GDP in March.

items.title: First Quarter GDP Growth Misses Expectations
items.summary: First quarter GDP grew at 2%, slightly below the expected 2.3%, with equipment investment in AI and computers along with consumer spending leading growth.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Banks and Crypto Industry Finalize Stablecoin Yield Deal, Paving Way for Clarity Act
items.summary: Senators Tillis and Brooks have finalized a compromise on stablecoin yield, opening the path for the Clarity Act to advance to markup and vote. This deal between banks and the crypto industry marks significant regulatory progress.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Surges Above $78,000 as MACD Signals Potential Bullish Flip
items.summary: Bitcoin is trading over $78,000 with the MACD on the daily chart showing signs of flipping bullish after a bearish fakeout, indicating bears losing momentum. Analysts warn of key resistance at 80K and potential drops to 74,500 or 69K if not broken.

items.title: Japan's Largest Exchange JPX to Launch Bitcoin and Crypto ETFs
items.summary: Japan's JPX announced plans to launch Bitcoin and crypto ETFs, contributing to global spot price pressure alongside US ETFs. Bitcoin closed April up nearly 12%, with predictions reaching $125,000.

items.title: Ethereum Processes Record 18.5 Million Transactions in One Week
items.summary: Ethereum mainnet handled 18.5 million transactions in the last 7 days, up 38% week-over-week and 110% year-over-year, marking its highest ever weekly volume.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta Plans 10% Workforce Cut Tied to AI Investments
items.summary: Mark Zuckerberg announced Meta's plan to reduce its workforce by about 10% to fund increased AI investments, while leaving room for further layoffs. This reflects broader industry shifts toward AI prioritization amid job cuts at Meta and Microsoft.

items.title: Elon Musk Sues OpenAI, Accusing Altman of Abandoning Nonprofit Mission
items.summary: Elon Musk's legal battle against OpenAI began with claims that Sam Altman transformed the nonprofit into a profit-driven entity. Musk expressed regret for originally backing OpenAI.

items.title: US Blocks Advanced AI Chip Tech Exports to China's Hua Hong
items.summary: The US has blocked advanced AI chip technology exports to China's Hua Hong amid concerns over national security and competition in AI hardware.

items.title: AI Magazine Highlights Top Stories Featuring NVIDIA, Google Cloud, OpenAI, Siemens
items.summary: AI Magazine's top five AI stories from the past few days spotlight major developments involving NVIDIA, Google Cloud, OpenAI, and Siemens.

items.title: Q1 GDP Growth Driven by AI Equipment and Computer Investments
items.summary: US first-quarter GDP grew 2%, below expectations, with equipment investment in AI and computers alongside consumer spending as key drivers.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: World of Warcraft Announces Patch 12.0.7 with New Content
items.summary: Blizzard has revealed details for WoW patch 12.0.7, including the return of Turbulent Timeways, a new one-boss raid featuring the fungal giant Rottme, and other features like Flex Mythic, amid discussions on the game's direction.

items.title: Roblox Announces Chat Updates Benefiting ERLC Roleplay Servers
items.summary: Roblox is testing new in-game chat features, including AI-generated chat summaries for users in different age groups, replacing the current lock system and improving access for roleplay servers like ERLC.

items.title: Solo Leveling: Arise Reveals Exchange Shop Nerfs and New Summoning Banner
items.summary: The game is revamping its exchange shop with token removals and other changes described as nerfs, alongside confirmation of a new anniversary summoning banner and additional buffs.

items.title: Regiments Receives Major New Update
items.summary: A significant update has launched for the singleplayer tactical game Regiments, highlighted in recent gameplay coverage sponsored by MicroProse.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Shares Rise on Strong Q3 Revenue Forecast
items.summary: Apple's stock rose after reporting a strong third-quarter revenue forecast with sales expected to increase 14-17%, despite warnings of rising memory-chip costs and ongoing Mac shortages. The company exceeded second-quarter expectations across the board.

items.title: Apple Discontinues Cheapest Mac Mini
items.summary: Apple has discontinued its entry-level Mac mini, with the compact desktop now starting at $799 and including 512GB of storage.

items.title: Roblox Shares Plunge on User Decline and Lowered Forecast
items.summary: Roblox reported fewer daily users in Q1 due to safety features, leading to a plunge in shares and a lowered full-year bookings forecast with projected further user declines.

items.title: Reddit Shares Gain on Strong Ad Revenue Projection
items.summary: Reddit's shares rose after projecting current-period sales surpassing Wall Street estimates, driven by surging advertising business growth.

items.title: Nissan Abandons US EV Plant Plans
items.summary: Nissan has scrapped plans for a US electric vehicle plant that was set to produce 200,000 EVs annually.

items.title: Europe and China Reach Electric Vehicle Tipping Point
items.summary: A 'tipping point' for electric vehicles has been reached in Europe and China, marking significant adoption in these markets.
